page: Microsoft Azure M416ds_6_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MDSHighMemoryv3 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture using Intel processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 416 dedicated vCPUs and a massive memory capacity of 5696.0 GB, providing approximately 13.69 GB of RAM per core.

For storage, the server includes a 410 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external storage volumes. There are no integrated GPUs on this instance. The hardware profile is designed for extreme resource density, prioritizing massive memory capacity and high vCPU counts over specialized hardware accelerators.
This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM and processing power.
description: Microsoft Azure M416ds_6_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MDSHighMemoryv3 family, designed for large-scale enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel processor on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides 416 dedicated vCPUs and 5696.0 GB of system memory, yielding 13.69 GB of RAM per core. The server includes a 410 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage for high-performance external disk configurations. It does not feature any GPU accelerators. This high-density resource profile is qualitatively positioned for massive memory-bound applications, making it highly suitable for large relational databases, in-memory data stores, and heavy enterprise resource planning systems.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M416ds_6_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 416 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 5696.0 GB of RAM, and a 410 GB local disk, designed for large enterprise databases and in-memory workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring massive RAM capacity and dedicated processing cores for enterprise-scale database and in-memory workloads.
